posts.author: ramansharma
posts.community: Yash High Voltage Ltd : Transformer Guardians
posts.text: It doesn’t seems to be concerning looking at the percentage w.r.t the total shareholding. They still own large chunk. Promoters have a track record of buying their shares from open market. Promoters have personal financial lives too like taxes, diversification, real estate, whatever. None of that means they’ve turned bearish on their own business.

posts.community: Yash High Voltage Ltd : Transformer Guardians
posts.text: Management recently sold 1.81L shares in open market - did not see any block deals here which means shares got offloaded to retail including HNIs - but this happened just days after management giving super good guidance and con call etc

posts.author: Manik_Gupta
posts.community: Yash High Voltage Ltd : Transformer Guardians
posts.text: It is expected that In forward 1.5 -2 years, revenue will get double as greenfield capacity units will get live. PAT margin % will definitely improve as greenfield capacity includes production of capacitive core which is currently being imported in India. Capacitive core is a one of the parts of RIP bushing which is more than 80% product mix of Yash Highvoltage.

posts.author: Debojit_Kangsa_Banik
posts.community: Yash High Voltage Ltd : Transformer Guardians
posts.text: The demand is quite strong for bushings. If they have necessary certificates in place and quality testing, then with a lower pricing, they should be able to gain market share. Remember the size of this company. Even a small market share gain will be huge profit for Yash. They have been marketing their brand quite well. They are quite active in social media Recent video on their manufacturing facility

posts.author: Rushin_Shah
posts.community: Yash High Voltage Ltd : Transformer Guardians
posts.text: Cracking large share in exports market using Indian made RIP core seems far fetched. Currently, exports markets are dominated by global as well as Chinese players which are having in-house developed and manufactured Core. Also, considering the criticality of bushing, global players would not shift to a new supplier just because it is 10-15% cheaper and a product which costs 5-8% of total transformer cost. (which is still very difficult because you are going to compete with Chinese players - most possible way is to seed exports market with selling products at significantly cheaper prices while…
